title: Ordered-boosting categorical specialist with raw IDs and pairwise race-lap context
summary: Your data has very high-cardinality Driver and race identifiers.
rationale: Your data has very high-cardinality Driver and race identifiers. CatBoost explicitly uses ordered categorical statistics to reduce target leakage, making it a strong complementary specialist when many entities are rare or unseen in test.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost model on mostly raw categorical columns plus a small set of numeric features and categorical crosses such as Driver x Compound, Race x Compound, and Race x Lap bucket. Keep all target-stat-like features out unless generated strictly out-of-fold; compare plain Logloss vs AUC metric tuning.
expected_effect: Often gives complementary rank gains over feature-heavy tree models by handling sparse categorical interactions more cleanly.
risk: If external preprocessing frequency/target encodings are built globally, leakage will erase any benefit. Rare unseen drivers may still make gains fragile.
